title: Translate brief set into an additional language
context: Following the first translation (i18n-015), this backlog task covers translating additional delivered briefs into a second target language to further widen the beneficiary reach. The specific language is determined by partner demand and reviewer availability. Each translation requires both a domain expert (for statistical accuracy) and a language reviewer (for non-alarmist fidelity), and all attribution, non-endorsement, and 'not advice' requirements must be preserved in the target language.
objective: Translate a set of delivered plain-language briefs into a second agreed target language with domain and language review, preserving the non-alarmist standard and all required notices.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one delivered brief translated into a target language different from the one covered in i18n-015, selected in consultation with the confirmed delivery partner and based on reviewer availability.
Translation reviewed by a domain expert confirming statistical accuracy and correct representation of all technical terms, indicator definitions, and uncertainty in the target language.
Translation reviewed by a language reviewer confirming the non-alarmist standard is maintained and no alarmist, politically-charged, or advice-implying framing was introduced in the target language.
Review artifacts committed (domain-review pass/flag, language-review pass/flag); no unresolved flags at publication.
FAO attribution, non-endorsement disclaimer, and 'not advice' notice preserved and correctly rendered in the target language.
Translated output committed alongside the English original, clearly labeled as an adaptation; commit is DCO signed-off.
output: Committed translated briefs in the second agreed target language, with domain and language review artifacts and all required notices preserved.
